steps.title: Calculate outer and inner radii
steps.content: The pipe has an outside diameter of 60 inches, so the outer radius $R = 30$ inches. The wall thickness is $\frac{5}{8}$ inches, so the inner radius $r = 30 - \frac{5}{8} = 29.375$ inches. The height $h = 144$ inches.

steps.title: Compute wall volume using annulus formula
Multiply by pi and select closest answer
steps.content: $V = \pi \times 5343.75 \approx 3.1416 \times 5343.75 \approx 16{,}788$ cubic inches. That matches choice D — D is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '177': Far too small; this would correspond to a very thin sliver with incorrect units or formula. ✗
  • Choice B — '565': Still far too small; does not account for the height of 144 inches. ✗
  • Choice C — '2,700': This is roughly π × 144 × 6, which overestimates the wall width or underestimates the pipe circumference. ✗
  • Choice D — '16,788': Using the annulus formula V = πh(R − r)(R + r) = π(144)(0.625)(59.375) ≈ 16,788 cubic inches. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
